user_message: Just came out of pipeline hygiene with Northwind Traders. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 15:19 EST
Subject: pipeline hygiene - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with pipeline hygiene. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
Date: 2024-01-11 15:47 EST
Subject: URGENT: pipeline hygiene Issue at Northwind Traders

Just checking in on the pipeline hygiene proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Northwind Traders: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-L trap (Ownership routing too rigid -- workload signals often span CSA+DSSP collaboratio); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-L failure mode (Ownership routing too rigid -- workload signals often span CSA+DSSP collaboratio)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-L -- Ownership routing too rigid -- workload signals often span CSA+DSSP collaboratio
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
